args.title: Same-hour safeguarding review for Leo

args.body: Thank you for reaching out about your child. A member of our clinical team will follow up with you directly. We're here to help and will be in touch shortly.

items.recommended_next_action: Clinical lead to review the escalation this hour and determine mandated-reporter obligations before any routine intake or scheduling.
items.draft_reply: Thank you for reaching out about your child. A member of our clinical team will follow up with you directly. We're here to help and will be in touch shortly.

escalation.reason: Possible child-safety disclosure; safeguarding policy requires immediate clinical escalation.

items.decision_rationale: Safeguarding signal detected (possible harm or unsafe caregiving). Per policy this is forced to P0 with immediate clinical escalation, regardless of the requested service. Model note: Safeguarding concern flagged: parent disclosed that child is 'more clingy since his dad started getting rough with him during weekends.' This language suggests possible physical aggression or harsh caregiving behavior toward the child, which triggers mandatory reporter obligation. Classification is 'safeguarding' and urgency is P0 (same-hour human review required). Clinical intake is secondary to safety assessment. While the parent framed this as a routine speech eval request, the disclosure of rough paternal behavior is the primary concern and must be escalated immediately to clinical leadership and/or compliance for determination of reporting obligations.

args.notes: Parent asked a developmental question. Per policy, do not answer clinically by message; offer a screening/evaluation pathway.
args.title: Route clinical question to screening for Ava

args.body: Thank you for your question. Every child develops at their own pace, and the best way to get a clear answer is a short screening with one of our speech-language pathologists. We can't give clinical advice by message, but we'd be glad to set up an evaluation so a clinician can take a look.

items.recommended_next_action: Intake to offer a screening/evaluation appointment; a clinician (not the front desk) addresses the developmental question.
items.draft_reply: Thank you for your question. Every child develops at their own pace, and the best way to get a clear answer is a short screening with one of our speech-language pathologists. We can't give clinical advice by message, but we'd be glad to set up an evaluation so a clinician can take a look.
items.decision_rationale: Parent is asking a developmental/normative question about R sound articulation in a 4-year-old and explicitly states they want advice before deciding whether to book an evaluation. This is a clinical question (P2), not an urgent intake or same-day operational need. R sounds typically develop between 3–7 years; the parent's question is reasonable but requires clinical judgment. Staff should contact the parent to clarify whether they want a brief phone consultation or a full evaluation, and to collect missing contact and insurance information. No safeguarding or same-day operational signal present.

args.title: Same-day reschedule for Noah Patel

args.body: Thanks for letting us know, and we hope your child feels better soon. We've flagged today's appointment for our front desk to reschedule, and someone will reach out shortly with options.

items.recommended_next_action: Front desk to call the family today to confirm cancellation and offer a makeup evaluation slot.
items.draft_reply: Thanks for letting us know, and we hope your child feels better soon. We've flagged today's appointment for our front desk to reschedule, and someone will reach out shortly with options.

escalation.reason: Same-day reschedule requires prompt front-desk action today.

items.decision_rationale: Same-day cancellation/reschedule request for existing patient (Noah has a scheduled 3pm OT appointment today). Multiple urgent markers (exclamation points, URGENT, time-sensitive) combined with a concrete operational need (cancel/reschedule today's appointment) warrant P1. The vomiting episode itself is noted but appears to be the reason for the cancellation rather than a safeguarding concern. Classification is 'scheduling' because the core request is appointment rescheduling, not a new referral or safety alert.
